lesson.title: Lesson 26: Wanted a Large Biscuit Tin

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: Lesson 26.

translation.text: 第26课。

segments.text: Wanted a Large Biscuit Tin.

translation.text: 征求一个大饼干筒。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Who won the prize for the biggest biscuit?

translation.text: 谁凭最大的饼干赢得了奖品？

segments.text: No one can avoid being influenced by advertisements.

translation.text: 没有人能避免受到广告的影响。

segments.text: Much as we may pride ourselves on our good taste, we are no longer free to choose the things we want, for advertising exerts a subtle influence on us.

translation.text: 尽管我们可能为自己的高雅品味而自豪，却已无法自由选择想要的东西，因为广告在潜移默化地影响着我们。

segments.text: In their efforts to persuade us to buy this or that product, advertisers have made a close study of human nature and have classified all our little weaknesses.

translation.text: 为了说服我们购买这种或那种产品，广告商深入研究了人性，并把我们所有的小弱点分门别类。

segments.text: Advertisers discovered years ago that all of us love to get something for nothing.

translation.text: 多年前广告商就发现，我们人人都喜欢不劳而获。

segments.text: An advertisement which begins with the magic word, free, can rarely go wrong.

translation.text: 以“免费”这个神奇词语开头的广告几乎从不会失败。

segments.text: These days, advertisers not only offer free samples, but free cars, free houses, and free trips around the world as well.

translation.text: 如今，广告商不仅赠送免费样品，还赠送汽车、房屋以及免费的环球旅行。

segments.text: They devise hundreds of competitions which will enable us to win huge sums of money.

translation.text: 他们设计出数百种竞赛，让我们有机会赢得巨额奖金。

segments.text: Radio and television have made it possible for advertisers to capture the attention of millions of people in this way.

translation.text: 广播和电视使广告商能够用这种方式吸引数百万人的注意。

segments.text: During a radio program, a company of biscuit manufacturers once asked listeners to bake biscuits and send them to their factory.

translation.text: 一家饼干制造公司曾在广播节目中请听众烘烤饼干并寄到工厂。

segments.text: They offered to pay ten dollars a pound for the biggest biscuit baked by a listener.

translation.text: 他们愿以每磅10美元的价格购买听众烤出的最大饼干。

segments.text: The response to this competition was tremendous.

translation.text: 这项竞赛引起了极其热烈的反响。

segments.text: Before long, of all shapes and sizes began arriving at the factory.

translation.text: 没过多久，各种形状和大小的饼干便开始送到工厂。

segments.text: One lady brought in a biscuit on a wheelbarrow.

translation.text: 一位女士用独轮车推来一块饼干。

segments.text: It weighed nearly five hundred pounds.

translation.text: 它重近500磅。

segments.text: A little later, a man came along with a biscuit which occupied the whole boot of his car.

translation.text: 不久，一个男人又带来一块占满汽车整个后备箱的饼干。

segments.text: All the biscuits that were sent were carefully weighed.

translation.text: 所有送来的饼干都经过仔细称重。

segments.text: The largest was seven hundred thirteen pounds.

translation.text: 最大的一块重713磅。

segments.text: It seemed certain that this would win the prize.

translation.text: 看来它肯定会获奖。

segments.text: But just before the competition closed, lorry arrived at the factory with a truly colossal biscuit which weighed two thousand four hundred pounds.

translation.text: 但就在竞赛截止前，一辆卡车载着一块真正巨大的饼干来到工厂，重达2,400磅。

segments.text: It had been baked by a college student who had used over one thousand pounds of flour, eight hundred pounds of sugar, two hundred pounds of fat, and four hundred pounds of various other ingredients.

translation.text: 它是一名大学生烤制的，使用了1,000多磅面粉、800磅糖、200磅油脂和400磅其他各种原料。

segments.text: It was so heavy that a crane had to be used to remove it from the lorry.

translation.text: 饼干太重，只得用起重机把它从卡车上卸下来。

segments.text: The manufacturers had to pay more money than they had anticipated, for they bought the biscuit from the student for twenty-four thousand dollars.

translation.text: 制造商支付的钱比预想的多，因为他们以24,000美元向这名学生买下了饼干。
